paper_title: On heroes in digraphs with forbidden induced forests
summary: The question of whether $\Delta(1,2,2)$ is a hero in $\{K_1+\vec{P}_2\}$-free digraphs (equivalently, whether $\{\Delta(1,2,2), K_1+\vec{P}_2\}$ is $\vec{\chi}$-finite) remains unresolved as of 2026. It is the sole unresolved case in the near-complete characterisation of heroes in this class given by Aboulker–Aubian–Charbit (Theorem 1.11 of the source paper) and extended to $\{H, rK_1+\vec{P}_3\}$-free digraphs by the source paper itself. No follow-up paper resolving this specific open problem was found in the indexed literature.
search_queries: Delta(1,2,2) hero digraph chi-finite Carbonero Koerts Moore Spirkl 2306.04710
heroes digraphs K1+P2-free chi-finite Delta(1,2,2) Aboulker Aubian Charbit
arxiv 2024 2025 heroes digraphs Delta(1,2,2) chi-finite K1+P2-free dichromatic number
notes: No follow-up found after 5 web calls (3 searches, 2 fetches). The most closely related recent paper is arXiv:2602.08736 (Claw,C3-free digraphs with unbounded dichromatic number, 2026), which improves on work by the same authors on a different conjecture (Aboulker–Charbit–Naserasr) and does not address Delta(1,2,2) in K1+P2-free digraphs.
